changes.detail: frontend/app/types/generated/openapi.ts is present and is the source of ApiAnalysisStartRequest/Response types (imported at analysis.ts line 1 via 'import type { components } from ./generated/openapi'). If any DTO shape changes are made under the bounded hardening gate, Cursor must run 'npm run generate-types' to regenerate this file. The file is covered by the 'potentially allowed' list under frontend/app/types/**/* but regeneration is a build step, not a manual edit. Cursor must verify whether the generate-types script exists and is current before making any DTO shape changes. If the script is absent or broken, TypeScript contract changes are blocked until it is resolved.

changes.check: Stage 2B — Intelligence Core components in scope identified
changes.detail: Intelligence Core components in scope for audit (read-only unless bounded hardening gate triggers): (1) backend/core/dto/builders.py — DTO output assembly (Line 30: build_analysis_result_dto); (2) backend/app/routes/analysis.py — API payload route; (3) backend/core/analytics/** — analytics components (arbitration_engine, balanced_systems_presentation_v1, domain_score_assembler, report_compiler_v1, etc.) to be identified in Phase 1 authority inventory; (4) backend/core/pipeline/orchestrator.py — pipeline orchestration. Primary outputs of this sprint are documentation files, not runtime changes.

changes.check: Stage 2B — Behavioural surface area and expected output changes
changes.detail: Expected output changes: NONE unless the Phase 2 audit gate is passed and bounded non-breaking DTO hardening is explicitly required. The sprint's primary outputs are four documentation files plus tests and Sentinel entries. Small non-breaking DTO hygiene changes are allowed ONLY if: frontend consumer impact is fully traced, TypeScript types updated, compatibility documented, regression tests added (prompt Phase 4 Implementation Limits). Any breaking DTO change requires STOP for GPT/human authority. Intentional output changes are bounded and gated.

Protected behaviours explicitly listed and test files confirmed present: LC-S8F/G unit and display fidelity, LC-S8D unit governance sentinel, LC-S10B launch-core protection, LC-S11A trust blockers, LC-S13 lifestyle/coherence/narrative, LC-S14 direction-aware scoring. Homocysteine lead finding is explicitly named as a protected behaviour in the required tests section. All 8 test files confirmed present.

The Phase 2 material mismatch STOP conditions are well-specified and cover the correct failure modes (majority fallback/boilerplate, fields not in DTO contract, governed assets unreachable, breaking contract changes required, unsupported clinical claims, internal governance string exposure). Cursor may not self-rescope if STOP triggers — must report and wait. This gate is correctly positioned before Phase 3 and Phase 4 implementation.

Forbidden files explicitly prohibit: backend/core/scoring/**, backend/core/units/**, backend/ssot/units.yaml, backend/ssot/scoring_policy.yaml, backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml, knowledge_bus/** (no new medical content), all automation_bus control-plane files, all kernel/gate scripts. The sprint scope is correctly bounded to: audit docs, tests/validators, Sentinel entries, and optionally bounded DTO hygiene under strict gate conditions. No path from authorised work to forbidden files is detectable.

Primary risk: if any DTO field classification triggers a small hardening change (e.g. field marked internal-only and removed from payload), that change must be deterministic, tested, and frontend-consumer-traced. The prompt's Implementation Limits section (Phase 4) correctly gates this. Audit-phase work (Phases 1-3) carries no determinism risk as it is read-only documentation. Documentation-only lifecycle controls must not be described as gates (correct instruction in prompt Phase 3). No silent output drift risk identified in the current prompt scope.

changes.check: Stage 2B — No-op check: real work exists
changes.detail: Not a no-op sprint. Real work exists: (1) No LC-S16 frontend-surface audit document exists yet (confirmed absent); (2) No LC-S17 Knowledge Bus lifecycle framework document exists; (3) No LC-S19 payload contract hardening notes exist; (4) DTO fields in builders.py are not yet formally classified by the categories required; (5) Knowledge Bus lifecycle states are not yet formally defined in a framework document; (6) 8 new Sentinel defect classes are required and not yet present. Sprint is fully justified.

verdict: HARDENED. All five front matter fields valid. risk_level HIGH and change_type MIXED correctly classified. All seven mandatory authority paths and all four optional paths confirmed present. All eight cross-sprint regression test files confirmed present. DTO authority confirmed singular (builders.py line 30). Frontend surface (17 result components) confirmed present and auditable. Sentinel packs directory has 3 existing files — 8 new defect classes required. One warning recorded: generated/openapi.ts must be regenerated via npm run generate-types if DTO shape changes are made; Cursor must verify script availability before any such change. No blockers. Cursor may proceed to Stage 3 kernel start.
